processes.objective: Write a TypeScript function that validates email addresses using a regex, with 3 unit tests using assert

processes.name: metacog-daemon

processes.name: awareness-daemon

blackboard.system:design-guidelines:
This system solves problems by spawning cognitive sub-processes.
The topology (which processes exist, how they coordinate) IS the algorithm.
Design the shape of the computation, then let processes execute it.
Key primitives: spawn, kill, fork, join, checkpoint, restore.
Blackboard is shared memory — write results there for other processes to read.
Observation is mandatory: produce → observe → proceed. Never assume success.

blackboard.architecture:plan-draft: Plan draft: implement a small TypeScript email validator function using a practical regex and add exactly 3 assert-based unit tests aligned with existing project test tooling. Waiting on scout:codebase for file locations/patterns, scout:dependencies for runner/toolchain, and scout:domain for regex/test-case guidance.

ephemeral:dependency-scout:eph-1fbcd67a-.name: dependency-scout
ephemeral:dependency-scout:eph-1fbcd67a-.response:
Direct result: TypeScript unit tests are already set up with Vitest; run them with `npm test` (script is `vitest run --passWithNoTests`).

Wrote findings to: `/Users/shawndavies/dev/cognitive-kernels/scout:dependencies`

## Key evidence
- Test script: `/Users/shawndavies/dev/cognitive-kernels/package.json:20-22`
  - `"test": "vitest run --passWithNoTests"`
- Vitest dependency: `/Users/shawndavies/dev/cognitive-kernels/package.json:37-42` (`vitest: 3.0.8`)
- ESM package: `/Users/shawndavies/dev/cognitive-kernels/package.json:5` (`"type": "module"`)
- Node engine: `/Users/shawndavies/dev/cognitive-kernels/package.json:23-25` (`node >=18`)
- TS config: `/Users/shawndavies/dev/cognitive-kernels/tsconfig.json:4-5` (`module/moduleResolution: NodeNext`)
- Test layout: `/Users/shawndavies/dev/cognitive-kernels/test/os/entry.test.ts:1` (Vitest import); tests live under `/Users/shawndavies/dev/cognitive-kernels/test/` as `*.test.ts`

## Useful commands
- Full suite: `npm test`
- Single file: `npx vitest run test/os/entry.test.ts` (or `npm test -- test/os/entry.test.ts`)

## Gotcha
- `tsc -p tsconfig.json` doesn’t include `test/**` (only `src/**` is included at `/Users/shawndavies/dev/cognitive-kernels/tsconfig.json:16`), so tests aren’t built/typechecked by `npm run build`—they still run fine via Vitest.

ephemeral:domain-scout:eph-630e8962-f59.name: domain-scout
ephemeral:domain-scout:eph-630e8962-f59.response:
scout:domain

## Best-practice (what to validate)
- OWASP: RFC email syntax is “far more complicated” than most realize; regex-only “proper parsing” is hard; recommended approach is basic syntactic checks + *semantic validation* by sending a verification email. Source excerpt lines: OWASP Input Validation Cheat Sheet HTML `Email Address Validation` section (lines 3405–3425, 3429–3434 of fetched page). Evidence: `/Users/shawndavies/dev/cognitive-kernels` curl output (see command results; key points at lines 3412–3414 and 3422–3425).
- OWASP also suggests length constraints: local part ≤ 63 and total ≤ 254 (lines 3422–3425).

## Minimal robust regex (pragmatic, widely used)
- WHATWG HTML spec defines “valid email address” for `<input type="email">` and provides a JS/Perl-compatible regex implementation: (HTML spec fetched `input.html`, lines 2189–2193)
```js
/^[a-zA-Z0-9.!#$%&'*+\/=?^_`{|}~-]+@[a-zA-Z0-9](?:[a-zA-Z0-9-]{0,61}[a-zA-Z0-9])?(?:\.[a-zA-Z0-9](?:[a-zA-Z0-9-]{0,61}[a-zA-Z0-9])?)*$/
```
- Note from the same spec: this is a “willful violation” of RFC 5322 for practicality (lines 2182–2185).

## Suggested TS usage
- Use the WHATWG regex as a `RegExp` literal (keeps escaping correct for `/`):  
  `const EMAIL_RE = /^[a-zA-Z0-9.!#$%&'*+\\/=?^_`{|}~-]+@[a-zA-Z0-9](?:[a-zA-Z0-9-]{0,61}[a-zA-Z0-9])?(?:\\.[a-zA-Z0-9](?:[a-zA-Z0-9-]{0,61}[a-zA-Z0-9])?)*$/;`
- Optional (OWASP-aligned) prechecks before regex: `trim()`, total length ≤ 254, local-part length ≤ 63.

## 3 unit-test cases (match the WHATWG regex)
- Valid: `simple@example.com`
- Valid: `user.name+tag_123@example.co.uk`
- Invalid: `user@-example.com` (domain label cannot start with `-`; fails regex)

ephemeral:codebase-scout:eph-6667de2a-81.name: codebase-scout
ephemeral:codebase-scout:eph-6667de2a-81.response:
Direct result: Wrote repo structure + test/import conventions to `/Users/shawndavies/dev/cognitive-kernels/scout:codebase`.

## Key findings (for email validator + 3 tests)
- Test runner is Vitest via `/Users/shawndavies/dev/cognitive-kernels/package.json:19-22` (`"test": "vitest run --passWithNoTests"`).
- Repo is ESM + NodeNext (`/Users/shawndavies/dev/cognitive-kernels/package.json:5`, `/Users/shawndavies/dev/cognitive-kernels/tsconfig.json:4-5`).
- Tests commonly import TS modules using `.js` extensions (NodeNext mapping), e.g. `/Users/shawndavies/dev/cognitive-kernels/test/os/entry.test.ts:35`.
- Existing tests use `describe/test/expect` from Vitest; mocking uses `vi.mock` + `vi.hoisted` (see `/Users/shawndavies/dev/cognitive-kernels/test/os/entry.test.ts:1-35`).
- No current usage of Node `assert` in tests (`rg "node:assert"` found no matches), but `import assert from "node:assert/strict";` inside Vitest tests should fit this ESM setup (noted in `scout:codebase`).
- Public export barrel is `/Users/shawndavies/dev/cognitive-kernels/src/index.ts:1-54` (only needed if the helper should be part of package API).
